mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at 0.08% (near zero) signals an extended monetary easing regime that depresses discount rates on equity cash flows. This persistently low rate environment produces sustained positive S&P 500 returns as long as FEDFUNDS remains below 1.0% over the 20-day forward horizon.
regime_hint: monetary easing cycle with near-zero rates
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of periods where FEDFUNDS < 1.0%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 12-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity. When UNRATE increases by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window, equity valuations compress due to lower expected corporate earnings, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances (UNRATE rise > 0.3pp), or correlation between UNRATE change and forward returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) at 626.8 billion dollars reflects consumer spending strength. A sustained decline in RSAFS of more than 2% month-over-month signals weakening consumer demand and reduced corporate profit expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as the market reprices earnings forecasts downward.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 626833.0 million as of June 2007
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances (RSAFS decline > 2%), or Spearman rank correlation between RSAFS momentum and forward returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) at 101.89 reflects manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side economic strength. When INDPRO declines by more than 1.5% over a 6-month window, it signals supply-side weakness and potential stagflation concerns, triggering negative equity returns as investors reduce growth expectations and increase risk premiums over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 65% of triggered instances (INDPRO decline > 1.5%), or the predictive power of INDPRO changes is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) jointly capture consumer and construction demand. When both HOUST falls below 1.5 million units annualized AND RSAFS growth turns negative, the combined signal indicates broad-based demand destruction. This interaction produces negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as the market reprices growth expectations downward.
candidate_trigger: HOUST < 1.5M units AND RSAFS month-over-month growth < 0%
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1679.0 thousand as of May 2007
fact:RSAFS at 626833.0 million as of June 2007
regime_hint: synchronized demand contraction across housing and retail
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 58% of triggered instances (both conditions met), or the joint effect of HOUST and RSAFS is not significantly stronger than either variable alone (interaction F-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) and unemployment rate (UNRATE) jointly characterize labor market momentum. When PAYEMS growth turns negative (month-over-month) AND UNRATE rises simultaneously, the interaction signals rapid labor market deterioration that triggers equity sell-offs as investors anticipate recession. This produces negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month growth < 0% AND UNRATE rises > 0.2pp
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 148951.0 thousand as of June 2007
regime_hint: rapid labor market deterioration signaling recession risk
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances (both conditions met), or the joint predictive power is not significantly greater than UNRATE alone (Vuong test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) and monetary policy (FEDFUNDS) interact to determine real interest rates and policy stance. When CPIAUCSL rises more than 0.4% month-over-month while FEDFUNDS remains below 1.0%, the central bank is effectively tightening in real terms despite nominal easing. This policy contradiction produces negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as investors recognize policy constraints.
regime_hint: stagflation risk with constrained monetary policy
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances (both conditions met), or the interaction term is not statistically significant in a regression model (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) represents aggregate demand strength. In late-cycle expansions (GDPC1 growth > 2.5% annualized), equity valuations are vulnerable to negative surprises. When GDPC1 growth decelerates below 2.0% annualized, the market reprices terminal growth rates downward, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as investors shift from expansion to slowdown expectations.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth rate decelerates below 2.0%
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 19478.893 billion chained 2017 dollars as of Q1 2007
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 62% of triggered instances (GDPC1 growth < 2.0%), or Rank IC between GDPC1 growth deceleration and forward returns is below 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.
